name: Bernie Glow
abstract: Bernie Glow (February 6, 1926 – May 8, 1982) was an American trumpet player who specialized in jazz and commercial lead trumpet from the 1940s to 1970s. He was born Bernard Abraham Glatzer on Feb 6 1926 in New York to Gustav (Gus) and Frances (Faye) Glatzer.

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio
Miles Davis with Gil Evans and Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: Bernie joined the band when I was at the . . . I think it was when . . . Roy was finished by then I believe. Yes, and Bernie was coming in to replace him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing dates with Bernie Glow and Mel Davis... Mel and Bernie and I were doing a thing for RCA Camden.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: We had eight brass, Bernie Glow, Goz, me and Bernie Glow, Markie and some other trumpet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: There’s Bernie Glow and Joe Wilder sitting in the trumpet section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: Most of the really good players would come in, like say their platform was four chairs for the trumpets, if it’s Doc Severinsen or Bernie Glow or anything, they wouldn’t go in and automatically sit in the middle.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: I’m talking about people like Phil Bodner say, the reed player, Bernie Glow, trumpeter... And my colleagues in the piano department were Bernie Leighton...
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]
